page: Microsoft Azure D3 is a D-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8171M processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 35 MB L3 cache. It includes hardware flags such as AVX, AVX-512F, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA.

The instance is equipped with 14.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.5 GB per core, and includes 205 GB of bundled local storage. It does not contain any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. In benchmark evaluations, the server exhibits generally weak to poor performance profiles. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng and Geekbench place the system in the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. Memory read bandwidth is also weak, though large block write bandwidth reaches average levels.

Given its hardware profile, the D3 is positioned as a general-purpose instance. It is suitable for workloads with moderate resource demands that can leverage its dedicated CPU allocation and bundled local storage, such as basic web serving, light database operations, or small-scale development environments. However, its low benchmark scores in compression, cryptography, and LLM inference suggest it is less optimal for compute-intensive or high-throughput data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D3 is a general-purpose virtual machine in the D family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8171M processor with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, the instance features 14.0 GB of RAM (3.5 GB per core) and 205 GB of bundled local storage, with no integrated GPUs. Benchmark results indicate generally weak performance, with single-core and multi-core CPU metrics, memory read bandwidth, and cryptographic operations mostly falling into the bottom 25% or bottom 10% of tested systems. Large block memory write bandwidth and small-model text generation represent areas of average performance. The D3 is best suited for entry-level general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, and lightweight database operations that require dedicated CPU resources but do not demand high-performance computing capabilities.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D3 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 14.0 GB of RAM, and 205 GB of local storage, designed for basic workloads and light database operations.

tagline: A general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, bundled local storage, and balanced memory allocation for basic workloads.

205 GB of bundled local storage with no integrated GPU
Average performance in large block memory write operations
